title: Outdoor Storage Bench — Vertical Slat Design
summary: A weatherproof storage bench with vertical-slat sides and a hinged lid — deck-box storage for cushions and toys that looks like furniture, not a plastic tub.
description:
This is a deck box that earns a spot on the patio: a slatted bench with a hinged lid that swallows cushions, pool toys, or garden gear, built from 1x4, 1x3, and 1x2 stock. The vertical-slat side panels are what give it its clean, modern look instead of the usual plastic-bin aesthetic.

The construction is panelized, which makes a big box manageable and — nicely — optionally knockdown. You build two side panels and two end panels as flat frames filled with slats, then bolt them into a box. The plan even lets you skip glue at assembly if you want to be able to take it apart later; use glue only where you want permanence. Build the side panels starting from a leg, marking 1-1/2" in for the frame, and keep the slat spacing even so the panels read as a set.

Because it lives outside and holds things that shouldn't sit in a puddle, use exterior door hinges for the lid and exterior screws throughout, and finish every piece. The slatted design also breathes, which helps cushions dry. A slatted or spaced bottom lets water drain rather than pool — worth doing if this sits anywhere it'll catch rain.

materials.name: Waterproof wood glue
materials.note: Optional at assembly if you want it to knock down.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut four legs at 20", 22 side slats at 17", twelve end panel slats at 17", six bottom at 47" and 13 top slats at 23-3/4" from the 1x4. Cut four side frame top/bottom at 45-1/2", four end frame sides at 16-1/4" and four end frame top/bottom at 17-1/4" from the 1x3. Cut four side frame ends at 11-1/4" and three top frame supports at 45-1/2" from the 1x2. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to 14 8 ft 1x4, four 8 ft 1x3 and two 8 ft 1x2. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Build the side panels
steps.body: Build the two side panels. The easiest way is to start with one of the legs, mark it 1-1/2" in for the frame (a 1x2 makes a handy gauge since it's 1-1/2" wide), and build out from there. Fill with vertical slats spaced evenly.

steps.title: Build the end panels
steps.body: The end panels are simpler frames of slats. Screw the frame boards together, lay the slats in, and fasten. Keep the slat spacing consistent with the sides.

steps.title: Assemble the box
steps.body: Attach the four panels into a box with 2" screws. Skip the glue if you want to be able to disassemble it later; use glue for a stronger permanent joint.

steps.title: Add the bottom
steps.body: Attach the bottom boards with 2" screws — glue for permanence, no glue if you want knockdown. Leave slight gaps between bottom boards so water drains rather than pools.

steps.title: Build and hinge the lid
steps.body: Build the top by fastening the 13 slats across the three 1x2 supports. Hinge it to the box with exterior door hinges so it opens as a lid.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Finish every piece (you likely already did each panel), fill exposed screws with paintable silicone, lightly sand, and add a topcoat. Confirm the lid swings freely and the box drains.

images.alt: Outdoor storage bench with vertical-slat sides and a hinged lid
